summary: Desktop visual capture of the Lumina landing homepage at its settled frozen frame: aurora hero, headline, console preview card, trusted-by strip, feature grid, metrics, and pricing.

content.objective: Verify the homepage renders its settled hero with the frozen aurora background, headline, call-to-action buttons, and the floating console preview card at desktop width.
actions: Wait for the hero background and page to finish rendering the settled frame
validations: The hero headline reads Run inference at the edge with total observability
A primary Start free button and a secondary Book a demo button are visible in the hero
The floating console preview card shows a p95 latency value of 23ms
A Trusted by teams shipping at the edge strip of company logos is visible below the hero
The metrics band shows exactly four figures with their values: 12.4B, 23ms, 99.98%, and 34+
The floating console preview card shows a requests / s value of 48,210

summary: Tablet-width visual capture of the homepage, verifying the responsive layout reflows correctly at 1024px.

content.objective: Verify the homepage renders correctly at tablet width, with the hero, navigation, and content sections reflowing to the narrower viewport.
actions: Wait for the page to finish rendering the settled frame at tablet width
validations: The hero headline and the Start free button are visible
The feature cards reflow into a narrower grid that fits the tablet width

summary: Mobile-width visual capture of the homepage, verifying the single-column responsive layout and the collapsed navigation at 375px.

content.objective: Verify the homepage renders correctly at mobile width, stacking content into a single column and collapsing the navigation into a hamburger button.
actions: Wait for the page to finish rendering the settled frame at mobile width
validations: The header shows a hamburger menu button instead of the full navigation links
The hero and content sections stack vertically in a single column

summary: Functional check: the Features link in the header scrolls the page to the six-card feature grid section.

content.objective: Verify that clicking the Features link in the top navigation moves the page to the Features section, demonstrating a positive in-page navigation scenario.
actions: Click the Features link in the top navigation menu
validations: The Features section is visible with the heading Everything you need to run models in production
A grid of six feature cards is shown, including Low-latency inference, GPU autoscaling, and Live tracing
The six feature card titles are exactly Low-latency inference, GPU autoscaling, Live tracing, Security and compliance, Global edge regions, and Versioned rollouts

summary: Functional check: the hero Start free button brings the Pricing section with its three plan cards into view.

content.objective: Verify that clicking the primary Start free button in the hero brings the Pricing section into view, demonstrating that the main call to action leads to the plans.
actions: Click the Start free button in the hero section
validations: The Pricing section is visible with the heading Start free. Scale when you ship.
Three plan cards are shown: Developer at $0, Team at $99, and Enterprise at Custom
The Team plan card is marked with a Most popular badge
The Developer plan button reads Start free, the Team plan button reads Get started, and the Enterprise plan button reads Contact sales

summary: Functional check: the Pricing link in the header scrolls the page to the Pricing section independently of the hero call to action.

content.objective: Verify that clicking the Pricing link in the top navigation moves the page to the Pricing section, demonstrating a positive in-page navigation scenario.
actions: Click the Pricing link in the top navigation menu
validations: The Pricing section is visible with the heading Start free. Scale when you ship.
The Developer, Team, and Enterprise plan cards each show a Get started, Start free, or Contact sales button
The three plans are named, in order, Developer priced $0 / month, Team priced $99 / month, and Enterprise priced Custom

summary: Mobile navigation: the hamburger button opens the full-width menu drawer with the navigation links and the Get started button.

content.objective: Verify that, on a phone-width viewport, clicking the hamburger menu button opens the mobile drawer containing the navigation links and the Get started call to action.
actions: Click the hamburger menu button in the header
validations: A full-width navigation drawer is open below the header
The drawer lists the links Platform, Features, Metrics, Pricing, and Docs
The drawer contains a Get started button
Clicking the Features link inside the open drawer closes the drawer
